record.field: BookStack is a self-hosted wiki and documentation platform that organizes knowledge like a library, with an opinionated structure that stays simple to use.

BookStack is a self-hosted wiki and documentation platform that organizes knowledge like a library, with an opinionated structure that stays simple to use.

Content is arranged into shelves, books, chapters and pages, giving teams a clear, navigable hierarchy for technical docs, internal knowledge bases and procedures. Built on PHP and Laravel, it offers both WYSIWYG and Markdown editing, full-text search and granular role-based permissions. It targets organizations that want a focused, easy-to-maintain knowledge base rather than a complex CMS.

Key features
  • Library-style organization: shelves, books, chapters and pages
  • WYSIWYG and Markdown editors with diagrams.net drawing integration
  • Full-text search across all content and granular role-based permissions
  • Authentication via LDAP, SAML2, OIDC and social logins
  • Page revisions, comments, cross-linking and a REST API
  • Export to PDF, HTML and Markdown, plus multi-language interface support
Best for

Teams and organizations that need a tidy, self-hosted documentation site or internal knowledge base — IT departments, support teams, software projects and enterprises wanting controlled access without the overhead of a heavy CMS.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_documentation'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_collaboration'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_cms'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_notes'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_search'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_identity')])]

record.field: services: bookstack: image: lscr.io/linuxserver/bookstack:24.12.1 restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' environment: - PUID=1000 - PGID=1000 - APP_URL=http://{{ domain }} - DB_HOST=bookstack-db - DB_USERNAME=mariadb - DB_PASSWORD={{ db_password }} - DB_DATABASE=bookstack - APP_KEY={{ app_key }} volumes: - ./config:/config depends_on: - bookstack-db container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network bookstack-db: image: mariadb:10.11 restart: unless-stopped environment: - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD={{ db_password }} - MYSQL_DATABASE=bookstack - MYSQL_USER=mariadb - MYSQL_PASSWORD={{ db_password }} volumes: - ./db_data:/var/lib/mysql container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_bookstack_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T bookstack-db sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d bookstack-db # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T bookstack-db sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
